title: Afternoon On A Hill
content:
I will be the gladdest thing
            Under the sun!
        I will touch a hundred flowers
            And not pick one.

        I will look at cliffs and clouds
            With quiet eyes,
        Watch the wind bow down the grass,
            And the grass rise.

        And when lights begin to show
            Up from the town,
        I will mark which must be mine,
            And then start down!
meaning:
Edna St. Vincent Millay's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Afternoon On A Hill"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
